vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.5.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Directive Flow: Replace Overview tab with live DAG visualization unifying vBRIEF items and specialist pipeline

plan.title: Directive Flow: DAG-centric issue overview with live lifecycle visualization
plan.author: human:eltmon+agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: The Overview tab shows disconnected cards (Agent, Cost, Services, Workspace, Reviewer Summary) with no sense of flow. The vBRIEF DAG exists as a separate static tab. The dot stepper hints at the pipeline but provides no detail. Agent work, quality gates, and resources are all disjointed.
narratives.Proposal: Unify everything into a single live DAG where vBRIEF items are enriched nodes and the specialist pipeline (VERIFY → REVIEW → TEST → MERGE → DONE) extends the graph as tail nodes. A resource dock on the right shows containers/agents/workspace. A summary strip on the bottom shows progress and critical path.
narratives.Constraint: Must build on existing ReactFlow/Dagre infrastructure. Must read from new .pan/ directory structure (PAN-967). Must not change the specialist pipeline flow or vBRIEF spec. Desktop dashboard only.
narratives.Risk: Large ReactFlow graphs with many nodes + live WebSocket updates could cause rendering performance issues. The enriched nodes are significantly heavier than the current simple DAG nodes.

narrative.Action: Build a new ReactFlow custom node type that renders vBRIEF items with: status dot + title, bead progress bar, agent assignment (model, runtime state), duration + cost, inline AC checklist (compact/expanded), last inspection result, difficulty/priority badges. Status-dependent rendering: pending=gray/dimmed, running=green/glow, completed=green-fill/collapsed, blocked=red. Extend the existing PlanDAG node but much richer.

subItems.title: All 6 status states render correctly with distinct visual treatment (pending, approved, running, completed, blocked, cancelled)
Running nodes show agent model name and runtime state inline
Acceptance criteria shown as compact count (3/4 AC) with expand to full checklist
Cost and duration shown per-item when data is available

items.title: Create quality gate node components (VERIFY, REVIEW, TEST, MERGE, DONE)

narrative.Action: Build 5 new ReactFlow custom node types for the specialist pipeline stages. VERIFY shows typecheck/lint/test substeps and attempt count. REVIEW shows 5 reviewer columns with per-reviewer status, round number, requeue count. TEST shows test-agent status and skip reason. MERGE shows mergeStep progress (8 substeps), queue position, CI checks, blocker reasons, and human gate call-to-action. DONE shows merged commit, total cost, duration. Each reads from ReviewStatus via the Zustand store.

subItems.title: VERIFY node shows typecheck/lint/test substeps with pass/fail/running indicators and attempt counter
REVIEW node shows 5 reviewer columns with per-role status (running/done) and round number
MERGE node prominently displays human gate approval call-to-action when readyForMerge=true
MERGE node shows granular mergeStep progress through all 8 substeps
DONE node shows merged commit SHA, total cost, and duration

items.title: Build the unified DAG graph builder (work items + gate nodes + edges)

narrative.Action: Create a function that takes a VBriefDocument + ReviewStatus + AgentState and produces the complete ReactFlow graph: all work item nodes from vBRIEF items, gate nodes (VERIFY → REVIEW → TEST → MERGE → DONE) appended after terminal work items (those with no outgoing blocks edges), all vBRIEF edges styled by type (blocks=solid red, informs=dashed blue, suggests=dotted purple, invalidates=solid orange), and transition edges from terminal items to VERIFY. Critical path highlighting in orange. Use Dagre TB layout with appropriate rank separation between work items and gate nodes.

subItems.title: Correctly identifies terminal work items (no outgoing blocks edges) and connects them to VERIFY gate
Critical path highlighted in orange across both work items and gate nodes
All 4 edge types render with distinct visual styles matching current PlanDAG conventions

items.title: Implement rework cycle backward edges and node re-activation

narrative.Action: When reviewStatus is 'failed' or 'blocked', or testStatus is 'failed', render a dashed red backward edge from the gate node to the relevant work item nodes. The work items re-activate with phase 'review-response' and their status reverts to running. Show the circuit breaker count (autoRequeueCount/3) on the REVIEW gate node. Handle up to 3 rework cycles visually.

subItems.title: Dashed red backward edge renders from failed/blocked gate to work items during rework
Circuit breaker count displayed on REVIEW node (e.g., 'Round 2 · 1/3 requeues')

items.title: Wire live WebSocket data into DAG nodes (agent state, review status, costs)

narrative.Action: Connect the DAG to the Zustand store so nodes update in real-time. Work item nodes read from agentsById, agentRuntimeById for agent assignment and runtime state. Gate nodes read from reviewStatusByIssueId for pipeline progress. Use domain events (agent.activity_changed, review.status_changed, etc.) to trigger re-renders of affected nodes only — not the entire graph. Implement selective node updates to avoid ReactFlow performance issues with large graphs.

subItems.title: Work item nodes update in real-time when agent phase, status, or model changes
Gate nodes update in real-time as verification, review, test, merge statuses change
Only affected nodes re-render on state changes, not the entire graph

narrative.Action: Create a collapsible right sidebar showing: Containers section (live status, CPU/memory from docker-stats), Agents tree (work-agent at top, review-agent with nested sub-reviewers matching issue tree pattern), Workspace section (path, branch, VS Code button, Stop button), Services section (Frontend/API links), Attach section (tmux command with copy), Cost section (total + breakdown by model and stage). Uses existing data from read model and resource endpoints. Collapse state persisted to localStorage.

subItems.title: Container section shows live status and memory usage for all issue containers
Agent section shows hierarchical tree with review sub-agents nested under review-agent
Dock collapses/expands with state persisted to localStorage

narrative.Action: Create a fixed bottom bar showing: overall progress (items completed/total), current stage indicator, critical path length, parallel lane count (independent chains in DAG), total cost with trend. Compact single-line display. Updates from same store data as the DAG.

subItems.title: Shows item completion count and current active stage
Shows critical path length from criticalPath() computation

narrative.Action: Add a +/- toggle button in the summary strip or resource dock that opens a resizable panel showing the worktree diff vs main. Reuse the existing DiffPanel component and DiffWorkerPoolProvider. Fetch from GET /api/agents/:agentId/diffs/vs-main. The diff panel slides up from the bottom or opens as a split, with the DAG resizing to accommodate.

subItems.title: Diff panel renders worktree diff using @pierre/diffs with syntax highlighting
Toggle button shows/hides diff panel, DAG resizes accordingly

items.title: Implement escape state overlays (BLOCKED, CANCELLED, circuit breaker)

narrative.Action: When stuck=true, show a yellow warning banner on the current active stage node with stuckReason and Unstick action. When issue is cancelled, dim entire graph and gray all nodes. When circuit breaker trips (autoRequeueCount >= 3), show red banner on REVIEW node with 'Human intervention needed' and action buttons.

subItems.title: Stuck state shows yellow banner with reason on the blocked stage node
Circuit breaker tripped shows red banner with human intervention CTA on REVIEW node

items.title: Replace OverviewTab with DirectiveFlow as the default view

narrative.Action: Replace the OverviewTab component content with the new DirectiveFlow component (DAG + resource dock + summary strip). Retain the existing tab structure (Overview, Activity, Costs, PRD, STATE, vBRIEF, Beads, PR/Diff, Discussions) but Overview now shows the directive. The separate vBRIEF tab retains the List and Raw JSON views as secondary views for debugging. Remove the ReviewerSummary section (absorbed into REVIEW gate node). Remove the old card grid (Agent, Cost, By Stage, Services, Attach, Actions, Workspace, Links). The dot stepper at the top can be kept as a compact redundant indicator or removed.

subItems.title: Overview tab renders the full DirectiveFlow DAG instead of the card grid
All information previously on Overview tab is accessible from the DAG or resource dock
Gate node actions (Review & Test, Sync, Stop, Approve Merge) work correctly and call existing API endpoints

items.title: Handle issues with no vBRIEF plan gracefully

narrative.Action: When an issue has no vBRIEF plan (legacy issues, manually started agents), show a simplified linear flow with just the gate nodes (VERIFY → REVIEW → TEST → MERGE → DONE) and a single 'WORK' node representing the agent. This degrades gracefully from the full DAG to a pipeline view.

subItems.title: Issues without a vBRIEF show a linear pipeline (WORK → VERIFY → REVIEW → TEST → MERGE) instead of empty graph
